stats.fact: GPT-5.5 scores 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, surpassing Claude Opus 4.7 (69.4%) by over 13 percentage points
GPT-5.5 narrowly beats Claude Mythos Preview (82.0%) on Terminal-Bench 2.0 — essentially a statistical tie
Gemini 3.1 Pro scores 68.5% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, trailing GPT-5.5 by 14.2 percentage points
GPT-5.5 reaches 58.6% on SWE-Bench Pro (real-world GitHub issue resolution); Claude Opus 4.7 leads at 64.3%
GPT-5.5 scores 84.9% on GDPval, which tests agentic knowledge work performance across 44 occupations
GPT-5.5 API pricing: $5/1M input tokens, $30/1M output tokens — double the GPT-5.4 price; effective cost increase ~20% after token efficiency gains
GPT-5.5 Pro API tier: $30/1M input tokens, $180/1M output tokens
GPT-5.5 supports a 1M token context window and uses fewer tokens than GPT-5.4 for the same Codex tasks
GPT-5.5 launched April 23, 2026, to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers
